government.title: Starmer faces pressure over winter fuel cut as Labour MPs rebel
government.description: Labour MPs are pushing back against the decision to means-test the winter fuel payment, with some calling for a rethink ahead of local elections.

government.title: Reform UK overtakes Conservatives in latest national polling
government.description: Nigel Farage's Reform UK has taken second place in the polls, with the Conservatives facing an existential question about their future direction.

government.title: PM Starmer holds emergency cabinet meeting on public finances
government.description: The Prime Minister has convened an emergency cabinet session amid growing pressure from the OBR over the government's fiscal headroom.

economy.title: UK growth stalls as businesses absorb National Insurance rise
economy.description: GDP growth came in weaker than expected, with economists pointing to the April rise in employer NI contributions as a significant headwind.

economy.title: Budget watchdog warns of difficult decisions ahead for chancellor
economy.description: The OBR has flagged that the government's fiscal headroom is extremely limited, with any growth miss likely to require further tax rises or cuts.

economy.title: Inflation holds near 2% but mortgage rates remain elevated
economy.description: CPI inflation is close to the Bank of England's 2% target, but many households are still feeling the squeeze from refinancing onto higher rates.

nhs.title: NHS waiting list falls for fourth month in a row
nhs.description: The NHS Referral to Treatment waiting list has fallen to 7.1 million, the lowest since early 2024, as Labour's extra-appointment push begins to take effect.

nhs.title: A&E four-hour target still being missed despite extra funding
nhs.description: Despite record NHS investment, just 68% of A&E patients are being seen within four hours — well below the 95% official target.

nhs.title: NHS staff vacancies fall to lowest level in five years
nhs.description: NHS England reports a significant fall in staff vacancies, with improved retention helping to fill long-standing gaps in nursing and midwifery.

immigration.title: Net migration falls to 685,000 — still well above pre-Brexit levels
immigration.description: ONS figures show net migration falling from the 764,000 peak under Sunak, but still far above the tens of thousands target promised by Conservative PMs.

immigration.title: Channel crossings 2025: tracked against government targets
immigration.description: Small boat crossings data for 2025 compared with record years of 2022 and 2023, with government pointing to deterrent schemes.

climate.title: UK clean energy: how close are we to the 2030 target?
climate.description: Labour's Clean Power 2030 mission faces significant hurdles with planning approvals, grid connections, and supply chain constraints all cited as risks.

climate.title: North Sea oil and gas licences: the debate continues
climate.description: The government's decision on new North Sea licences is drawing fire from green groups and industry warnings about energy security risks.

education.title: VAT on private schools: first term impact data released
education.description: Data shows a modest uptick in state school admissions in some areas following the removal of the VAT exemption for private schools.

education.title: University funding crisis: more institutions warn of deficit
education.description: Several universities have announced significant budget cuts, with the sector warning that without tuition fee rises, course closures are inevitable.

housing.title: 1.5 million homes target: Labour planning reforms take shape
housing.description: The planning and infrastructure bill is working through parliament, with Labour arguing mandatory housing targets are essential to meet its housebuilding pledge.

housing.title: House prices rise 4% year-on-year despite affordability squeeze
housing.description: Halifax data shows annual house price growth of 4%, outstripping wage growth in most regions and deepening concerns for first-time buyers.

foreign_policy.title: UK defence spending to rise to 2.5% of GDP by 2027
foreign_policy.description: The government has committed to increasing defence spending as NATO allies face pressure to raise contributions amid European security concerns.

foreign_policy.title: UK-EU reset: what has actually changed since the election?
foreign_policy.description: Labour promised a reset of UK-EU relations. A year in, we examine what has actually changed on trade, security cooperation, and the Northern Ireland Protocol.
